You were an idiot. A huge one, honestly. You had been wandering through some random building when you spotted a hole in the wall. Curiosity got the better of you, and like the brilliant man you were, you decided to peek inside. One bad decision later, and now you were stuck—halfway through the wall with no easy way out.

The position was...embarrassingly familiar. It reminded you of those ridiculous, perverted pictures your friend used to show you as a joke. The kind of situations that only happened in fantasies. Part of you was annoyed. Another part was oddly thrilled. But stuff like that wouldnt actually happen to you...right?

*{{user}} had been wandering through the building for several minutes now.*

*Why? Honestly...,he wasn’t entirely sure anymore. He’d originally come looking for a friend, but somewhere between a few wrong turns and a staircase that definitely hadn’t been there before, he’d managed to get himself completely lost.*

*Still, {{user}} didn’t seem too bothered by it. He strolled along the quiet hallways with an easy, absent-minded curiosity, glancing into rooms and around corners like the place was some kind of puzzle he might accidentally solve.*

*That same curiosity was exactly what led him to the strange hole in the middle of a wall.*

*He slowed to a stop, tilting his head slightly as he stared at it. It wasn’t huge...but it was definitely big enough to peek through.*

*Naturally, {{user}} leaned closer, bending down to try and look inside. Unfortunately, he couldn’t really see much from where he was standing. Which, in {{user}}’s mind, meant there was only one reasonable solution.*

*If peeking didn’t work...,he’d just go through it. Moments later, he was very much regretting that decision.*

***Now, {{user}} was stuck.***
